title: Tundra thaw-window payoff pass
summary: Make Tundra short-season play feel observable and consequential after the late-season north-end pass.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: Tundra replay or active-window behavior changes what the player seeks, while the visual/copy load stays calm.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Harden world-state and request-state interactions around thaw-window active/inactive surfaces.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Tighten one Tundra relationship cluster around thaw, sedge/willow, wet tuft, or brief fruit under strict copy budgets.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Tune one Tundra physical cue or recovery lane that reinforces thaw-aware movement without adding a new route.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Deepen Thaw Window replay/active consequence through existing process and support seams.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.

recommended_snapshot.reason: A debug-only loadable state during peak Tundra thaw lets reviewers prove station, map, journal, and route-board surfaces show the active Thaw Window process rather than the baseline inactive handoff or later High Pass closure.

expected_state: biomeVisits.tundra at least 2 for thaw-fringe process activation
no route-marker, tundra-survey-slice, treeline-low-fell, forest-season-threads, High Pass, filed-season archive, save-schema, station-shell, or route-controller change
expected_assertions: guidedFieldSeason.stage settled with nextBiomeId tundra and THAW WINDOW station note
activeFieldRequest id tundra-short-season in tundra with process-backed Thaw Window title
route board remains TREELINE SHELTER LINE targeting tundra with tundra-short-season active and launchCard null
route board summary and replayNote text use Peak thaw makes first bloom, wet tuft, and brief fruit easiest to follow today.
world map current/focused location is tundra with routeMarkerLocationId null and Today: Thaw Window footer
field station routes page keeps hand-lens / HAND LENS selected and seasonWrap TODAY uses peak-thaw text
preserve: src/engine/field-requests.ts and Route v2 slot order
process focus copy and support targeting
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "route board to tundra|thaw-window route replay"

lane_1_main_result.summary: Added the debug-only tundra-thaw-window save snapshot and focused resolver/runtime tests proving the active peak-thaw Thaw Window state across station, map, journal, and route-board replay-note surfaces.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "route board to tundra|thaw-window route replay"
direct Playwright tundra-thaw-window snapshot proof with no console or page errors

snapshot serializes as current plain SaveState JSON and stays debug/test/docs only

lane_1_critic_result.summary: Confirmed the tundra-thaw-window snapshot is debug/test/docs only, round-trips as plain SaveState JSON, and proves active peak-thaw Thaw Window station, map, journal, and replay-note surfaces without High Pass or route-marker drift.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "route board to tundra|thaw-window route replay"
scope_preserved: no route definitions, process-focus copy, support targeting, station layout, world-map focus priority, save schema, Tundra geometry, High Pass copy, or route-controller behavior changed for this lane-1 pass
lane_1_critic_result.coordination_note: The shared worktree contains unrelated dirty files from other lane work, so do not bundle this packet into a lane-clear commit unless the tree is cleaned or coordinated.

recommended_target.reason: This existing note already links first bloom, cloudberry, and snow-bunting, making it the safest journal-facing place to give the route's brief-fruit payoff more memory without touching Thaw Window route behavior.

recommended_copy.summary: First blooms, birds, and cloudberry fruit all race the short tundra summer.
recommended_copy.observationPrompt: What here races the short summer?

preserve: tundra-short-season route definition, slot order, routeV2Note, processFocus, display prefix, and filed-note behavior
Tundra geometry, spawn ratios, process moments, ledger rows, close-look cards, sketchbook notes, comparison branches, and new species
recommended_tests: Add or extend resolver coverage for purple-saxifrage + cloudberry + snow-bunting resolving short-summer-rush
Assert the summary contains cloudberry fruit, race, and short tundra summer
Assert the prompt exactly matches What here races the short summer?
Rely on content-quality for the ecosystem-note budget ceiling

lane_3_scout_refinement.recommended_scope: Proof-first guard for the existing Tundra thaw-aware movement and recovery chain.
current_state: Tundra already has authored snow-meadow drift, thaw-skirt shelf/bench/channel, frost-ridge drift, and meltwater-bank recovery pieces.
Lane 1, lane 2, and lane 4 have already proved the active Thaw Window state, copy, and note-tabs payoff without needing more geometry.
The best lane-3 value is to connect the existing platforms and wet-ground carriers into one explicit physical-memory guard before adding density.
main_acceptance: add one test-only guard in `src/test/tundra-biome.test.ts` that pins `snow-meadow-drift-rest`, `thaw-skirt-entry-heave`, `thaw-skirt-upper-shelf`, `thaw-skirt-bench-rest`, `thaw-skirt-bank-shoulder`, `thaw-skirt-exit-heave`, `frost-ridge-drift-rest`, `meltwater-snow-lip`, and `meltwater-bank-rest` as one thaw-aware movement/recovery chain

assert the upper shelf sits above entry/bench, the thaw-skirt bench/shoulder/exit form a forgiving descending recovery lane, and the frost-ridge/meltwater pieces remain an existing north-end handoff rather than a new branch
keep the north-end proof inside the existing pocket by preserving `meltwater-bank-rest` before `x 604`

do not change Tundra geometry, rendering, route/support behavior, station UI, save schema, world-map behavior, science copy, journal/atlas copy, player physics, High Pass copy, or new UI surfaces unless a real failing proof is found
baseline_verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Thaw Window|thaw-window|tundra process|thaw fringe|thaw-hold|tundra survey place-tab"
main_verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Thaw Window|thaw-window|tundra process|thaw fringe|thaw-hold|tundra survey place-tab"
